AuditTemplate.description:
A named set of audit columns applied during data shaping.

Attributes:
    columns: Mapping of column names to Spark SQL expressions. Each entry
        defines a column that will be appended to the output dataset.

Example:
    >>> template = AuditTemplate(
    ...     columns={
    ...         "created_at": "current_timestamp()",
    ...         "created_by": "current_user()",
    ...     }
    ... )

columns.description: Mapping of column names to Spark SQL expressions. Each entry defines a column that will be appended to the output dataset.
columns.title: Columns

AuditTemplate.title: AuditTemplate

ColumnSet.description:
A named column set that defines an external column mapping.

Column sets describe where to find a mapping of incoming column names to
outgoing column names.  The mapping data can come from a Delta table, a
YAML file, or a runtime notebook parameter.  Exactly one of ``source`` or
``param`` must be provided.

Attributes:
    source: Source definition pointing to the mapping data.
    param: Name of a runtime notebook parameter that supplies the mapping
        at execution time.
    on_unmapped: Behaviour when an input column has no mapping entry.
        ``"pass_through"`` keeps the column unchanged; ``"error"`` aborts.
    on_extra: Behaviour when the mapping contains entries for columns not
        present in the input. ``"ignore"`` silently skips; ``"warn"`` logs
        a warning; ``"error"`` aborts.
    on_failure: Behaviour when the column set cannot be resolved (e.g.
        source unavailable). ``"abort"`` raises; ``"warn"`` logs and
        continues; ``"skip"`` silently skips the rename step.

source.description: Source definition pointing to the column mapping data.

param.description: Name of a runtime notebook parameter that supplies the mapping at execution time.
param.title: Param

on_unmapped.description: Behaviour when an input column has no mapping entry. ``pass_through`` keeps it unchanged; ``error`` aborts.
on_unmapped.title: On Unmapped

on_extra.description: Behaviour when the mapping contains entries for columns not present in the input. ``ignore`` skips silently; ``warn`` logs a warning; ``error`` aborts.
on_extra.title: On Extra

on_failure.description: Behaviour when the column set cannot be resolved (e.g. source unavailable). ``abort`` raises; ``warn`` logs and continues; ``skip`` silently skips the rename step.
on_failure.title: On Failure

ColumnSet.title: ColumnSet

ColumnSetSource.description:
Source definition for a named column set.

Describes where the column mapping data lives — either a Delta table or a
YAML file. Delta sources reference a registered table alias; YAML sources
reference a file path relative to the project root.

Attributes:
    type: Source kind — ``"delta"`` or ``"yaml"``.
    alias: Registered table alias. Required for ``type="delta"``.
    path: File path to the YAML mapping file. Required for ``type="yaml"``.
    from_column: Column name in the source that holds the incoming column
        names. Defaults to ``"source_name"``.
    to_column: Column name in the source that holds the outgoing (renamed)
        column names. Defaults to ``"target_name"``.
    filter: SQL WHERE expression applied when reading a Delta source.

type.description: Source kind — ``delta`` for a registered Delta table or ``yaml`` for a file.
type.title: Type

alias.description: Registered table alias. Required when ``type`` is ``delta``.
alias.title: Alias

path.description: File path to the YAML mapping file. Required when ``type`` is ``yaml``.
path.title: Path

from_column.description: Column name in the source that holds the incoming (original) column names.
from_column.title: From Column

to_column.description: Column name in the source that holds the outgoing (renamed) column names.
to_column.title: To Column

filter.description: SQL WHERE expression applied when reading a Delta source.
filter.title: Filter

ColumnSetSource.title: ColumnSetSource

ConditionSpec.description:
A condition expression for conditional execution.

Attributes:
    when: Condition expression string. Supports parameter references
        (``${param.x}``), built-in checks (``table_exists()``,
        ``table_empty()``, ``row_count()``), and simple boolean operators.

when.description: Condition expression string. Supports parameter references (${param.x}), built-in checks (table_exists(), table_empty(), row_count()), and simple boolean operators.
when.title: When

ConditionSpec.title: ConditionSpec

DedupConfig.description: Deduplication configuration applied immediately after reading a source.

order_by.description: Spark SQL ORDER BY expression to determine which row to keep when duplicates are found.
order_by.title: Order By

DedupConfig.title: DedupConfig

ExecutionConfig.description:
Runtime execution settings that cascade through loom/weave/thread.

Attributes:
    log_level: Logging verbosity for execution output.
    trace: Whether to collect execution spans for telemetry.

trace.description: Whether to collect execution spans for telemetry.
trace.title: Trace

ExecutionConfig.title: ExecutionConfig

LogLevel.description:
Configurable log level for weevr execution.

Controls the verbosity of structured logging output during pipeline
execution. Maps to Python logging levels internally.
LogLevel.title: LogLevel

LogMessageStep.description:
A log message emitted as a hook step.

Attributes:
    type: Step type discriminator, always ``"log_message"``.
    name: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
    on_failure: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the
        phase-specific default.
    message: Message template to log. Supports ``${var.name}`` placeholders.
    level: Log level for the message.

name.description: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
name.title: Name

on_failure.description: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the phase-specific default.
on_failure.title: On Failure

message.description: Message template to log. Supports ``${var.name}`` placeholders.
message.title: Message

LogMessageStep.title: LogMessageStep

Lookup.description:
A weave-level named data definition that can be referenced by threads.

Lookups define small reference datasets shared across threads in a weave.
When ``materialize`` is true, the data is read once and cached (or broadcast)
before threads execute.

Narrow lookup fields (``key``, ``values``, ``filter``) control projection
and filtering applied during materialization or on-demand reads.  When set,
only the declared key and value columns are retained, reducing memory and
improving join performance.

Attributes:
    source: Source definition for the lookup data.
    materialize: Whether to pre-read and cache/broadcast the data before
        thread execution.
    strategy: Materialization strategy. Only meaningful when ``materialize``
        is true. ``"broadcast"`` uses Spark broadcast join hints;
        ``"cache"`` uses DataFrame caching.
    key: Column(s) used for matching (join key). Kept in the cached
        projection alongside ``values``.
    values: Payload column(s) to retrieve. When set, only ``key`` + ``values``
        columns are retained in the cached DataFrame.
    filter: SQL WHERE expression applied to the source before projection.
    unique_key: When true, validate that ``key`` columns form a unique key
        after filtering and projection.
    on_failure: Behavior when ``unique_key`` check finds duplicates.
        Only meaningful when ``unique_key`` is true.

source.description: Source definition for the lookup data.

materialize.description: Whether to pre-read and cache or broadcast the data before thread execution.
materialize.title: Materialize

strategy.description: Materialization strategy when ``materialize`` is true. ``broadcast`` uses Spark broadcast join hints; ``cache`` uses DataFrame caching.
strategy.title: Strategy

key.description: Column(s) used for matching (join key). Retained in the cached projection alongside ``values``.
key.title: Key

values.description: Payload column(s) to retrieve. When set, only ``key`` + ``values`` columns are retained in the cached DataFrame.
values.title: Values

filter.description: SQL WHERE expression applied to the source before projection.
filter.title: Filter

unique_key.description: When true, validate that ``key`` columns form a unique key after filtering.
unique_key.title: Unique Key

on_failure.description: Behavior when the ``unique_key`` check finds duplicates. Only meaningful when ``unique_key`` is true.
on_failure.title: On Failure

Lookup.title: Lookup

NamingConfig.description:
Configuration for naming normalization.

Attributes:
    columns: Pattern to apply to column names. None means inherit from parent.
    tables: Pattern to apply to table names. None means inherit from parent.
    exclude: Glob patterns or explicit names to exclude from column normalization.
    on_collision: Behaviour when two columns normalise to the same name.
        ``"suffix"`` appends a numeric suffix to the later column; ``"error"``
        aborts with a validation error.
    reserved_words: Optional configuration for handling SQL reserved word
        collisions in column names. None means no reserved word handling.

columns.description: Pattern to apply to column names. None means inherit from parent.

tables.description: Pattern to apply to table names. None means inherit from parent.

exclude.description: Glob patterns or explicit names to exclude from column normalization.
exclude.title: Exclude

on_collision.description: Behaviour when two columns normalise to the same name. ``suffix`` appends a numeric suffix to the later column; ``error`` aborts with a validation error.
on_collision.title: On Collision

reserved_words.description: Optional configuration for handling SQL reserved word collisions in column names.

NamingConfig.title: NamingConfig

NamingPattern.description:
Column and table naming patterns.

Attributes:
    SNAKE_CASE: ``http_status``
    CAMEL_CASE: ``httpStatus``
    PASCAL_CASE: ``HttpStatus``
    UPPER_SNAKE_CASE: ``HTTP_STATUS``
    TITLE_SNAKE_CASE: ``Http_Status``
    TITLE_CASE: ``Http Status``
    LOWERCASE: ``httpstatus``
    UPPERCASE: ``HTTPSTATUS``
    KEBAB_CASE: ``http-status``
    NONE: Opt-out sentinel — no normalization applied.
NamingPattern.title: NamingPattern

OneLakeConnection.description:
A OneLake connection declaration.

Identifies the Fabric workspace and lakehouse that a source or target
resolves against at execution time.

workspace.description: OneLake workspace GUID or variable reference.
workspace.title: Workspace

lakehouse.description: OneLake lakehouse GUID or variable reference.
lakehouse.title: Lakehouse

default_schema.description: Default schema for tables in this connection.
default_schema.title: Default Schema

OneLakeConnection.title: OneLakeConnection

ParamSpec.description: Typed parameter specification for config-level param declarations.

name.description: Unique name of the parameter as referenced in config.
name.title: Name

type.description: Expected type of the parameter value.
type.title: Type

required.description: Whether the parameter must be supplied at runtime.
required.title: Required

default.description: Default value used when the parameter is not supplied and required is false.
default.title: Default

description.description: Human-readable description of the parameter's purpose.
description.title: Description

ParamSpec.title: ParamSpec

QualityGateStep.description:
A quality gate check executed as a hook step.

The ``check`` field determines which check-specific fields are required.

Attributes:
    type: Step type discriminator, always ``"quality_gate"``.
    name: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
    on_failure: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the
        phase-specific default (pre=abort, post=warn).
    check: Which quality gate check to perform.
    source: Table alias for ``source_freshness`` and ``table_exists`` checks.
    max_age: Duration string for ``source_freshness`` (e.g. ``"24h"``).
    target: Table alias for ``row_count_delta`` and ``row_count`` checks.
    max_decrease_pct: Max allowed decrease percentage for ``row_count_delta``.
    max_increase_pct: Max allowed increase percentage for ``row_count_delta``.
    min_delta: Minimum absolute row change for ``row_count_delta``.
    max_delta: Maximum absolute row change for ``row_count_delta``.
    min_count: Minimum row count for ``row_count``.
    max_count: Maximum row count for ``row_count``.
    sql: Spark SQL boolean expression for ``expression`` check.
    message: Failure message for ``expression`` check diagnostics.

name.description: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
name.title: Name

on_failure.description: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the phase-specific default (pre=abort, post=warn).
on_failure.title: On Failure

check.description: Which quality gate check to perform.
check.title: Check

max_decrease_pct.description: Maximum allowed row count decrease percentage for ``row_count_delta``.
max_decrease_pct.title: Max Decrease Pct

max_increase_pct.description: Maximum allowed row count increase percentage for ``row_count_delta``.
max_increase_pct.title: Max Increase Pct

min_delta.description: Minimum absolute row change allowed for ``row_count_delta``.
min_delta.title: Min Delta

max_delta.description: Maximum absolute row change allowed for ``row_count_delta``.
max_delta.title: Max Delta

min_count.description: Minimum row count threshold for the ``row_count`` check.
min_count.title: Min Count

max_count.description: Maximum row count threshold for the ``row_count`` check.
max_count.title: Max Count

sql.description: Spark SQL boolean expression for the ``expression`` check.
sql.title: Sql

QualityGateStep.title: QualityGateStep

ReservedWordConfig.description:
Configuration for handling reserved word collisions in column names.

When a column or table name matches a reserved word, the engine can
handle the collision in one of three ways: quote the name, prefix it,
or raise an error.

The ``preset`` field selects one or more built-in word lists. When
omitted, the ANSI SQL list is used as the default. Specifying any
preset replaces that default — to include ANSI words alongside other
presets, list ``"ansi"`` explicitly. A single string is accepted as
shorthand for a one-element list.

The ``extend`` and ``exclude`` lists compose on top of the resolved
preset union, adding or removing individual words.

Attributes:
    strategy: How to handle reserved word collisions. ``"quote"`` wraps
        the name in back-ticks; ``"prefix"`` prepends ``prefix``;
        ``"error"`` raises a validation error.
    prefix: String prepended to colliding column names when
        ``strategy="prefix"``.
    preset: Built-in word list presets to activate. ``None`` (default)
        uses the ANSI SQL list. Accepts a single string or a list.
    extend: Additional words to treat as reserved beyond the preset.
    exclude: Words to remove from the reserved word check.

strategy.description: How to handle reserved word collisions. ``quote`` wraps in back-ticks; ``prefix`` prepends ``prefix``; ``error`` raises a validation error.
strategy.title: Strategy

prefix.description: String prepended to colliding column names when ``strategy`` is ``prefix``.
prefix.title: Prefix

preset.description: Built-in word list presets to activate. ``None`` uses the ANSI SQL list. Accepts a single string or a list.
preset.title: Preset

extend.description: Additional words to treat as reserved beyond the resolved preset.
extend.title: Extend

exclude.description: Words to remove from the reserved word check.
exclude.title: Exclude

ReservedWordConfig.title: ReservedWordConfig

ReservedWordPreset.description:
Built-in reserved word list presets.

Each preset represents a self-contained set of reserved words for a
specific query language or engine context. Presets can be combined
via list composition; the effective word set is the union.

Attributes:
    ANSI: ANSI SQL reserved keywords (~80 words).
    DAX: DAX reserved words for Power BI semantic models.
    M: M language (Power Query) reserved words.
    POWERBI: Convenience alias expanding to DAX + M union.
    TSQL: T-SQL reserved keywords for Fabric SQL endpoints.
ReservedWordPreset.title: ReservedWordPreset

Source.description:
A data source declaration.

A source is either a direct data reference (with ``type``), a connection-based
reference (with ``connection`` + ``table``), a lookup reference (with ``lookup``),
or a generated sequence (with ``type`` set to ``date_sequence`` or
``int_sequence``). ``lookup`` is mutually exclusive with all other resolution modes.

Cross-field validation rules:

- If ``lookup`` is set: ``type`` must not be set.
- If ``connection`` is set: ``table`` must be set; ``alias`` must not be set;
  ``type`` must be None or ``"delta"``; ``connection`` sources resolve via the
  named connection rather than an inline alias.
- ``table`` without ``connection`` is rejected.
- ``connection`` and ``alias`` are mutually exclusive.
- If neither ``lookup`` nor ``connection`` is set: ``type`` is required.
- ``type == "delta"`` without ``connection`` requires ``alias`` to be set.
- ``type`` in file types (csv, json, parquet, excel) requires ``path`` to be set.
- Generated types (``date_sequence``, ``int_sequence``) require ``column``,
  ``start``, and ``end``; ``alias``, ``path``, ``dedup``, ``connection``, and
  non-empty ``options`` are rejected.
- ``date_sequence`` step must be one of ``day``, ``week``, ``month``, ``year``,
  or omitted.
- ``int_sequence`` step must be a positive integer or omitted.

type.description: Source type: delta, csv, json, parquet, excel, date_sequence, or int_sequence.
type.title: Type

alias.description: Registered table alias. Required for delta sources without a connection.
alias.title: Alias

path.description: File path. Required for file-based sources (csv, json, parquet, excel).
path.title: Path

dedup.description: Deduplication configuration applied after reading the source.

lookup.description: Reference to a named lookup defined at the weave or loom level. Mutually exclusive with type.
lookup.title: Lookup

connection.description: Reference to a named connection defined at thread, weave, or loom level.
connection.title: Connection

schema.description: Schema override within the connection's lakehouse.
schema.title: Schema

table.description: Table name within the connection's lakehouse.
table.title: Table

start.description: Sequence start value. Required for generated types. Accepts a date string (date_sequence) or integer (int_sequence).
start.title: Start

end.description: Sequence end value (inclusive). Required for generated types. Accepts a date string (date_sequence) or integer (int_sequence).
end.title: End

column.description: Output column name. Required for generated types.
column.title: Column

step.description: Sequence step. For date_sequence: one of day, week, month, year. For int_sequence: a positive integer. Defaults to 1 (int_sequence) or day (date_sequence) when omitted.
step.title: Step

Source.title: Source

SqlStatementStep.description:
An arbitrary SQL statement executed as a hook step.

Optionally captures the scalar result into a weave-scoped variable
via ``set_var``.

Attributes:
    type: Step type discriminator, always ``"sql_statement"``.
    name: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
    on_failure: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the
        phase-specific default.
    sql: Spark SQL statement to execute.
    set_var: Optional variable name to capture the scalar result into.

name.description: Optional name for telemetry span naming.
name.title: Name

on_failure.description: Failure behaviour. ``None`` means the executor applies the phase-specific default.
on_failure.title: On Failure

set_var.description: Optional variable name to capture the scalar result into.
set_var.title: Set Var

SqlStatementStep.title: SqlStatementStep

ThreadEntry.description:
A thread reference within a weave, with optional per-thread overrides.

Attributes:
    name: Thread name. Required for inline definitions; derived from
        filename stem for external references.
    ref: Path to an external ``.thread`` file, relative to project root.
        Mutually exclusive with inline definition (name + body).
    as_: Alias that overrides the thread's effective name for all downstream
        consumers (planner, executor, telemetry, watermark, display).
        Required when the same ref appears multiple times in a weave.
    params: Key-value dict injected into the thread's parameter resolution
        context under the ``param`` namespace, enabling ``${param.x}``
        references in the thread config.
    dependencies: Explicit upstream thread names. When set, these are merged
        with any auto-inferred dependencies from source/target path matching.
    condition: Optional condition for conditional execution. When set, the
        thread is only executed if the condition evaluates to True.

name.description: Thread name. Required for inline definitions; derived from filename stem for external references.
name.title: Name

ref.description: Path to an external .thread file, relative to project root. Mutually exclusive with inline definition.
ref.title: Ref

as.description: Alias that overrides the thread's effective name. Required when the same ref appears multiple times in a weave.
as.title: As

params.description: Key-value dict injected into the thread's parameter context under the param namespace, enabling ${param.x} references.
params.title: Params

dependencies.description: Explicit upstream thread names. Merged with auto-inferred dependencies from source/target path matching.
dependencies.title: Dependencies

condition.description: Optional condition for conditional execution. Thread runs only if the condition evaluates to True.

ThreadEntry.title: ThreadEntry

VariableSpec.description:
Declaration of a weave-scoped variable.

Variables are typed scalar values that can be set by hook steps via
``set_var`` and referenced in downstream config as ``${var.name}``.

Attributes:
    type: Scalar type of the variable value.
    default: Optional default value used when no hook sets the variable.

type.description: Scalar type of the variable value.
type.title: Type

default.description: Optional default value used when no hook sets the variable.
default.title: Default

VariableSpec.title: VariableSpec

description: A collection of thread references with optional shared defaults.

name.description: Weave name, derived from filename stem for external references.
name.title: Name

qualified_key.description: Dot-separated namespace key, derived from directory path.
qualified_key.title: Qualified Key

threads.description: Ordered list of thread references in this weave.
threads.title: Threads

defaults.description: Default values inherited by all threads in this weave.
defaults.title: Defaults

params.description: Typed parameter declarations with defaults and validation.
params.title: Params

naming.description: Naming normalization rules for columns and tables.

lookups.description: Named reference datasets shared across threads in this weave.
lookups.title: Lookups

column_sets.description: Named external column mappings for bulk rename operations.
column_sets.title: Column Sets

variables.description: Typed variable declarations set by hook steps via set_var.
variables.title: Variables

pre_steps.description: Hook steps executed before threads in this weave run.
pre_steps.title: Pre Steps

post_steps.description: Hook steps executed after all threads in this weave complete.
post_steps.title: Post Steps

audit_templates.description: Named audit column templates inherited by threads.
audit_templates.title: Audit Templates

connections.description: Named connection definitions for resolving source and target paths.
connections.title: Connections

title: Weave
